assumptions: The analysis is for consolidated financial statements.
The recognised intragroup dividend receivable is a monetary item that gives rise to foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of the intragroup monetary receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is a genuine hedge-accounting route because IFRS 9 allows a recognised asset to be designated in a fair value hedge and specifically permits foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item in consolidation when the FX effects are not fully eliminated.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of the intragroup monetary receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is a genuine hedge-accounting route because IFRS 9 provides for cash flow hedges of recognised assets or liabilities exposed to risk-related cash flow variability, subject to the intragroup monetary-item exception and the usual designation and effectiveness requirements.
